chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يَنْسَى الشَّهَادَةَ وَ يَعْرِفُ خَطَّهُ بِالشَّهَادَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 5 – The man forgets the testimony but recognises his handwriting with the testimony

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/224)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/224)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الرَّجُلُ يُشْهِدُنِي عَلَى الشَّهَادَةِ فَأَعْرِفُ خَطِّي وَ خَاتَمِي وَ لَا أَذْكُرُ شَيْئاً مِنَ الْبَاقِي قَلِيلًا وَ لَا كَثِيراً قَالَ فَقَالَ لِي إِذَا كَانَ صَاحِبُكَ ثِقَةً وَ مَعَكَ رَجُلٌ ثِقَةٌ فَاشْهَدْ لَهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Al Numan, from Hammad Bin Usman, from Umar Bin Yazeed who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The man made me a witness upon the testimony. So I recognised my handwriting and my seal, and did not remember anything from the remainder, neither less nor more’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘When it was such that your companions was reliable, and with you is a man who is reliable, so testify for him’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali ibn al-Nu’man from Hammad ibn ‘Uthaman from ‘Umar ibn Yazid who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who asks me to testify for a case and I recognize my handwriting and seal but do not remember anything of the rest, little or more. He (the Imam) said, ‘If the party is trustworthy and there is another trustworthy man, you can testify.’”
